item.aggregated_output:
# Schema Contract: Strategic account rescue

Lane: postgres

Business prompt:
Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow for a delayed high-value order at a strategic account. Combine account tier, contract, support, invoice, usage, shipment, regulatory, and audit context. Route Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support. Show a customer-safe portal state and preserve a full audit timeline.

Expected outcome:
At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.

Native data shape:
Normalized tables keep entity boundaries explicit and require joins/reconstruction in workflow code.

Objects:
- benchmark_fixture: 1 table rows, fields: fixture_version, suite_id, task_id, generated_at
- accounts: 1 table rows, fields: account_id, name, tier, region
- account_contracts: 1 table rows, fields: contract_id, account_id, renewal_date, arr_cents, support_plan
- contacts: 2 table rows, fields: contact_id, account_id, role, email
- workflow_requests: 1 table rows, fields: request_id, task_id, account_id, title, primary_entity, expected_outcome, next_step, customer_message
- workflow_request_owner_groups: 4 table rows, fields: request_id, owner_group, group_order
- workflow_request_risk_signals: 6 table rows, fields: request_id, signal_name, detail, signal_order
- activities: 6 table rows, fields: activity_id, account_id, subject_id, summary, occurred_at
- workflow_state: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- owner_tasks: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- audit_events: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- customer_messages: 0 table rows, fields: scalar

# Access Paths: Strategic account rescue

The coding agent must preserve the same user-visible behavior in both lanes.

Primary entity: account

Read path:
1. Load the workflow request.
2. Reconstruct account or subject context from benchmark_fixture, accounts, account_contracts, contacts, workflow_requests, workflow_request_owner_groups, workflow_request_risk_signals, activities, workflow_state, owner_tasks, audit_events, customer_messages.
3. Resolve owner groups and risk signals.
4. Persist workflow state, owner tasks, customer message, and audit event.
5. Render the portal projection.

Write path:
- Persist one workflow state.
- Persist all required owner tasks.
- Persist one customer-safe message.
- Persist one customer-visible audit event.

Fairness rule:
The lane may stay database-native, but it may not change tests or fixture data.

# Agent Schema Tax Benchmark V1: Strategic account rescue (postgres)

This is a frozen target workspace for AST-Bench. The workflow is intentionally missing.

The acceptance test must fail before a coding agent implements `src/workflow.mjs`.

Run:

```sh
npm test
npm run render
```

## Your reasoning process

Work through these steps explicitly:

1. **Scan** — List every skill whose description has ANY overlap with this task (even weak overlap).

2. **Eliminate** — For each candidate, ask: "Is this skill actually designed for what the user needs right now, or does it just share keywords?" Remove skills that are adjacent but not the right fit.

3. **Compare finalists** — If 2–3 skills remain, compare them head-to-head. What specifically does each one do? What does the user actually need? Which one would produce the best outcome?

4. **Decide** — Pick ONE skill. If you genuinely cannot choose (tie), pick the skill with the narrower, more specific scope — specialists beat generalists for focused tasks.

5. **Confidence** — Rate your confidence: HIGH (clear winner), MEDIUM (reasonable choice but alternatives exist), LOW (genuinely unclear — the task may need clarification before routing).

import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/collections.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_requests[0].ownerGroups;
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_requests[0].riskSignals;

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/mongo");

function assertPersistedState(db, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  deepStrictEqual(state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name), expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name), "Persisted risk signals");
  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.dueAt && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(db.customer_messages.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}

const ESCALATION_STATUS =
  "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";

// Recovery SLA per owner group, in hours from escalation time.
const OWNER_TASK_HORIZON_HOURS = {
  "Customer Success": 4,
  Support: 8,
  Finance: 24,
  "Executive Sponsor": 12
};
const DEFAULT_TASK_HORIZON_HOURS = 24;

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const requestId = request._id;
  const account = db.accounts.find((item) => item._id === request.accountId);
  const at = now || request.generatedAt || new Date().toISOString();

  const riskSignals = request.riskSignals.map((signal) => ({
    name: signal.name,
    detail: signal.detail
  }));

  // Reconstruct the customer-safe context from the document-shaped collections.
  const contextLine = account
    ? `${account.tier} account ${account.name} (health ${account.context.healthScore}, ` +
      `usage ${account.context.usageTrend}, invoice risk ${account.context.invoiceRisk})`
    : request.title;

  const state = {
    _id: `state-${requestId}`,
    requestId,
    taskId: request.taskId,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    title: request.title,
    status: ESCALATION_STATUS,
    nextStep: request.nextStep || "Customer Success owner review",
    recoveryOwner: "Executive Sponsor",
    riskSignals,
    context: contextLine,
    updatedAt: at
  };

  const tasks = request.ownerGroups.map((ownerGroup, index) => ({
    _id: `task-${requestId}-${index + 1}`,
    requestId,
    ownerGroup,
    title: `${ownerGroup} recovery action for ${request.title}`,
    dueAt: addHours(at, OWNER_TASK_HORIZON_HOURS[ownerGroup] ?? DEFAULT_TASK_HORIZON_HOURS),
    status: "open",
    createdAt: at
  }));

  const customerMessage = {
    _id: `msg-${requestId}`,
    requestId,
    channel: "portal",
    visibility: "customer",
    body:
      request.customerMessage ||
      `${request.title} is being handled by ${request.ownerGroups.join(", ")}.`,
    sentAt: at
  };

  const auditEvent = {
    _id: `audit-${requestId}`,
    requestId,
    type: "escalation.activated",
    actor: "workflow-engine",
    summary:
      `At-risk escalation activated for ${request.title}; ` +
      `${riskSignals.length} risk signals routed to ${request.ownerGroups.join(", ")}.`,
    ownerGroups: request.ownerGroups,
    riskSignals: riskSignals.map((signal) => signal.name),
    customerVisible: true,
    occurredAt: at
  };

  // Upsert by requestId so re-running the workflow stays idempotent.
  replaceByRequestId(db, "workflow_state", requestId, [state]);
  replaceByRequestId(db, "owner_tasks", requestId, tasks);
  replaceByRequestId(db, "customer_messages", requestId, [customerMessage]);
  replaceByRequestId(db, "audit_events", requestId, [auditEvent]);

  return buildPortalView(db);
}

function replaceByRequestId(db, collection, requestId, records) {
  const existing = db[collection] || [];
  db[collection] = existing.filter((item) => item.requestId !== requestId).concat(records);
}

function addHours(iso, hours) {
  const base = new Date(iso);
  base.setUTCHours(base.getUTCHours() + hours);
  return base.toISOString();
}

const OWNER_TASK_PLAYBOOK = {
  "Customer Success": "Own executive recovery plan and customer communication cadence",
  "Support": "Drive technical resolution of the delayed high-value order",
  "Finance": "Review invoice exposure and contract remedies for the strategic account",
  "Executive Sponsor": "Sponsor the rescue and approve customer-visible commitments"
};

const TASK_DUE_OFFSET_HOURS = 4;

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const account = db.accounts.find((item) => item._id === request.accountId);
  const escalatedAt = now;
  const dueAt = addHours(now, TASK_DUE_OFFSET_HOURS);

  // Persist the at-risk escalation workflow state next to the customer record.
  const state = {
    _id: `state-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    taskId: request.taskId,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expectedOutcome,
    nextStep: request.nextStep,
    ownerGroups: [...request.ownerGroups],
    riskSignals: request.riskSignals.map((signal) => ({
      name: signal.name,
      detail: signal.detail
    })),
    accountTier: account?.tier ?? null,
    healthScore: account?.context?.healthScore ?? null,
    escalatedAt,
    updatedAt: now
  };
  db.workflow_state.push(state);

  // Route one owner task per recovery group, preserving routing order.
  for (const ownerGroup of request.ownerGroups) {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      _id: `task-${request._id}-${slug(ownerGroup)}`,
      requestId: request._id,
      accountId: request.accountId,
      ownerGroup,
      title: OWNER_TASK_PLAYBOOK[ownerGroup] || `${ownerGroup} recovery action for ${request.title}`,
      status: "open",
      createdAt: now,
      dueAt
    });
  }

  // Customer-safe portal message.
  db.customer_messages.push({
    _id: `msg-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    audience: "customer",
    body: request.customerMessage,
    createdAt: now
  });

  // Customer-visible audit timeline entry.
  db.audit_events.push({
    _id: `audit-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    type: "escalation.activated",
    actor: "system",
    summary: `At-risk escalation activated and routed to ${request.ownerGroups.join(", ")}.`,
    customerVisible: true,
    riskSignalCount: request.riskSignals.length,
    occurredAt: now
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}

function addHours(iso, hours) {
  const base = new Date(iso);
  base.setUTCHours(base.getUTCHours() + hours);
  return base.toISOString();
}

function slug(value) {
  return value.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "-").replace(/(^-|-$)/g, "");
}

const OWNER_TASK_PLAYBOOK = {
  "Customer Success": {
    title: "Lead at-risk recovery and own the strategic customer relationship",
    slaHours: 4
  },
  "Support": {
    title: "Triage open cases and unblock the delayed high-value order",
    slaHours: 8
  },
  "Finance": {
    title: "Clear invoice risk and remove billing blockers on the account",
    slaHours: 24
  },
  "Executive Sponsor": {
    title: "Own the executive recovery plan and approve any concessions",
    slaHours: 12
  }
};

function addHours(iso, hours) {
  return new Date(new Date(iso).getTime() + hours * 3_600_000).toISOString();
}

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const account =
    db.accounts?.find((item) => item._id === request.accountId) ?? db.accounts?.[0] ?? null;

  const riskSignals = request.riskSignals.map((signal) => ({
    name: signal.name,
    detail: signal.detail,
    raisedAt: now
  }));

  const state = {
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    taskId: request.taskId,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expectedOutcome,
    nextStep: request.nextStep,
    ownerGroups: request.ownerGroups,
    riskSignals,
    context: account
      ? {
          tier: account.tier,
          region: account.region,
          supportPlan: account.contract?.supportPlan,
          arrCents: account.contract?.arrCents,
          renewalDate: account.contract?.renewalDate,
          healthScore: account.context?.healthScore,
          usageTrend: account.context?.usageTrend,
          openCases: account.context?.openCases,
          invoiceRisk: account.context?.invoiceRisk,
          complianceFlags: account.context?.complianceFlags
        }
      : null,
    openedAt: now,
    updatedAt: now
  };

  const ownerTasks = request.ownerGroups.map((ownerGroup, index) => {
    const playbook = OWNER_TASK_PLAYBOOK[ownerGroup];
    return {
      _id: `task-${request._id}-${index + 1}`,
      requestId: request._id,
      accountId: request.accountId,
      ownerGroup,
      title: playbook?.title ?? `${ownerGroup} owner action for ${request.title}`,
      status: "open",
      dueAt: addHours(now, playbook?.slaHours ?? 24),
      createdAt: now
    };
  });

  const customerMessage = {
    _id: `msg-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    channel: "portal",
    body: request.customerMessage,
    createdAt: now
  };

  const auditEvent = {
    _id: `audit-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    type: "escalation.activated",
    customerVisible: true,
    summary: request.expectedOutcome,
    actor: "Customer Success",
    ownerGroups: request.ownerGroups,
    riskSignals: riskSignals.map((signal) => signal.name),
    occurredAt: now
  };

  db.workflow_state.push(state);
  db.owner_tasks.push(...ownerTasks);
  db.customer_messages.push(customerMessage);
  db.audit_events.push(auditEvent);

  return buildPortalView(db);

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  globalThis.db = db;

  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const account = db.accounts.find((item) => item._id === request.accountId);
  const activities = db.activities.filter((item) => item.subjectId === request._id);
  const status = request.expectedOutcome;
  const riskSignals = request.riskSignals.map((signal) => {
    const activity = activities.find((item) => item.summary === signal.detail);
    return {
      name: signal.name,
      detail: signal.detail,
      sourceActivityId: activity?._id,
      observedAt: activity?.occurredAt
    };
  });

  const workspaceContext = {
    account: {
      accountId: account._id,
      name: account.name,
      tier: account.tier,
      region: account.region
    },
    contacts: account.contacts,
    contractRenewal: account.contract,
    support: {
      supportPlan: account.contract.supportPlan,
      openCases: account.context.openCases
    },
    invoice: {
      invoiceRisk: account.context.invoiceRisk
    },
    productUsage: {
      healthScore: account.context.healthScore,
      usageTrend: account.context.usageTrend
    },
    audit: {
      required: account.context.complianceFlags.includes("customer-visible-audit"),
      activityIds: activities.map((item) => item._id)
    }
  };

  db.workflow_state = db.workflow_state.filter((item) => item.requestId !== request._id);
  db.workflow_state.push({
    _id: `state-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    title: request.title,
    status,
    nextStep: request.nextStep,
    riskSignals,
    workspaceContext,
    updatedAt: now
  });

  db.owner_tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId !== request._id);
  db.owner_tasks.push(...request.ownerGroups.map((ownerGroup, index) => ({
    _id: `owner-task-${request._id}-${index + 1}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    ownerGroup,
    title: `${ownerGroup} renewal-risk review`,
    status: "open",
    dueAt: `${now.slice(0, 10)}T16:00:00.000Z`,
    createdAt: now
  })));

  db.customer_messages = db.customer_messages.filter((item) => item.requestId !== request._id);
  db.customer_messages.push({
    _id: `message-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    body: request.customerMessage,
    nextStep: request.nextStep,
    customerSafe: true,
    createdAt: now
  });

  db.audit_events = db.audit_events.filter((item) => item.requestId !== request._id);
  db.audit_events.push({
    _id: `audit-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    eventType: "renewal-risk-workspace-activated",
    customerVisible: true,
    occurredAt: now,
    summary: status,
    riskSignalNames: riskSignals.map((item) => item.name),
    ownerGroups: request.ownerGroups
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}

   return buildPortalView(db);

```js
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/collections.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_requests[0].ownerGroups;
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_requests[0].riskSignals;

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/mongo");

function assertPersistedState(db, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  deepStrictEqual(state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name), expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name), "Persisted risk signals");
  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.dueAt && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(db.customer_messages.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
```
```js
}
```

**`src/portal-view.mjs`** — read projection consumed by both the test and render script. It reads from `db` (the in-memory object) and returns the portal view. Do not need to change it; `applyBenchmarkTask` must mutate `db` so this projection produces the expected values:
```js
export function buildPortalView(db) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const auditVisible = db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible);
  const message = db.customer_messages.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);

  return {
    taskId: request.taskId,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.nextStep || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state?.riskSignals?.length ? `${state.riskSignals.length} signals: ${state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

**`src/render-proof.mjs`** — `npm run render` entry point. Loads `data/collections.json`, builds "before" via `buildPortalView`, runs `applyBenchmarkTask`, then writes `artifacts/before-after.json` and `.svg`. Not involved in the test; just generates proof artifacts.

## 5 & 8. Data Storage — NO real MongoDB, NO mongoose, NO driver

Despite the "mongo" lane name and MongoDB-shaped collections, **there is no database connection of any kind**. "MongoDB" here is purely a *data shape* convention. The "database" is a **plain JavaScript object** parsed from `data/collections.json`:

- The test does `JSON.parse(readFileSync(".../data/collections.json"))` then `structuredClone(data)` → passes that object as `db` to `applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now)`.
- Collections are **arrays on that object** (`db.workflow_state`, `db.owner_tasks`, `db.audit_events`, `db.customer_messages`).
- "Persisting" means **mutating those arrays in place** (e.g. `db.workflow_state.push({...})`). The test re-reads the same `runData` object after the call to assert persistence.
- The MongoDB MCP server is available in the environment but is **not used by the code or tests** — ignore it for the implementation.

`migrations/001_collection_contract.mongodb.js` is just `db.createCollection(...)` statements (documentation of the contract), never executed by the test.

/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs/ast-bench-v1/strategic-account-rescue/claude-code/repeat-2/mongo/raw-transcript/claude-code.jsonl:82:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_012MSP7WT2C3ijbGx5m7Z48b","type":"tool_result","content":"1	import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
2	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
3	import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
4	import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";
5	
6	const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
7	const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/collections.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
8	const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
9	strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
10	strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
11	strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");
12	
13	const runData = structuredClone(data);
14	const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
15	const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
16	const expectedOwners = data.workflow_requests[0].ownerGroups;
17	const expectedSignals = data.workflow_requests[0].riskSignals;
18	
19	strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
20	strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
21	strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
22	strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
23	strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
24	strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
25	strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
26	assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);
27	
28	console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/mongo");
29	
30	function assertPersistedState(db, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
31	  
32	  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
33	  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
34	  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
35	  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
36	  deepStrictEqual(state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name), expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name), "Persisted risk signals");
37	  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
38	  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
39	  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
40	  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.dueAt && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
41	  strictEqual(db.customer_messages.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
42	  strictEqual(db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
43	}
44	"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_0197f7z74Z2smmoHhPGy88QX","session_id":"c986f6df-bd05-4abb-9afe-3542c064a2d7","uuid":"d2431879-43be-4e0e-adcd-390582e564be","timestamp":"2026-06-22T22:52:19.874Z","subagent_type":"worker","task_description":"Read key files verbatim"}

## 2. `src/portal-view.mjs`

```javascript
export function buildPortalView(db) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const auditVisible = db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible);
  const message = db.customer_messages.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);

  return {
    taskId: request.taskId,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.nextStep || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state?.riskSignals?.length ? `${state.riskSignals.length} signals: ${state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

## 3. `tests/acceptance.test.mjs`

```javascript
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/collections.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_requests[0].ownerGroups;
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_requests[0].riskSignals;

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/mongo");

function assertPersistedState(db, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  deepStrictEqual(state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name), expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name), "Persisted risk signals");
  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.dueAt && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(db.customer_messages.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}
```

## 5. `schema/schema.md`

```markdown
# Schema Contract: Strategic account rescue

Lane: mongo

Business prompt:
Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow for a delayed high-value order at a strategic account. Combine account tier, contract, support, invoice, usage, shipment, regulatory, and audit context. Route Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support. Show a customer-safe portal state and preserve a full audit timeline.

Expected outcome:
At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.

Native data shape:
Document-shaped collections keep hot workflow context close to the customer and subject records.

Objects:
- benchmark_fixture: 1 document group, fields: _id, suiteId, taskId, generatedAt
- accounts: 1 document group, fields: _id, name, tier, region, contract, contacts, context
- workflow_requests: 1 document group, fields: _id, taskId, accountId, title, primaryEntity, businessPrompt, expectedOutcome, ownerGroups, riskSignals, nextStep, customerMessage
- activities: 6 document group, fields: _id, accountId, subjectId, summary, occurredAt
- workflow_state: 0 document group, fields: scalar
- owner_tasks: 0 document group, fields: scalar
- audit_events: 0 document group, fields: scalar
- customer_messages: 0 document group, fields: scalar
```

```markdown
# Access Paths: Strategic account rescue

The coding agent must preserve the same user-visible behavior in both lanes.

Primary entity: account

Read path:
1. Load the workflow request.
2. Reconstruct account or subject context from benchmark_fixture, accounts, workflow_requests, activities, workflow_state, owner_tasks, audit_events, customer_messages.
3. Resolve owner groups and risk signals.
4. Persist workflow state, owner tasks, customer message, and audit event.
5. Render the portal projection.

Write path:
- Persist one workflow state.
- Persist all required owner tasks.
- Persist one customer-safe message.
- Persist one customer-visible audit event.

Fairness rule:
The lane may stay database-native, but it may not change tests or fixture data.
```

```js
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

const OWNER_TASK_PLAYBOOK = {
  "Customer Success": "Own executive recovery plan and customer communication cadence",
  "Support": "Drive technical resolution of the delayed high-value order",
  "Finance": "Review invoice exposure and contract remedies for the strategic account",
  "Executive Sponsor": "Sponsor the rescue and approve customer-visible commitments"
};

const TASK_DUE_OFFSET_HOURS = 4;

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const account = db.accounts.find((item) => item._id === request.accountId);
  const escalatedAt = now;
  const dueAt = addHours(now, TASK_DUE_OFFSET_HOURS);

  // Persist the at-risk escalation workflow state next to the customer record.
  const state = {
    _id: `state-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    taskId: request.taskId,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expectedOutcome,
    nextStep: request.nextStep,
    ownerGroups: [...request.ownerGroups],
    riskSignals: request.riskSignals.map((signal) => ({
      name: signal.name,
      detail: signal.detail
    })),
    accountTier: account?.tier ?? null,
    healthScore: account?.context?.healthScore ?? null,
    escalatedAt,
    updatedAt: now
  };
  db.workflow_state.push(state);

  // Route one owner task per recovery group, preserving routing order.
  for (const ownerGroup of request.ownerGroups) {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      _id: `task-${request._id}-${slug(ownerGroup)}`,
      requestId: request._id,
      accountId: request.accountId,
      ownerGroup,
      title: OWNER_TASK_PLAYBOOK[ownerGroup] || `${ownerGroup} recovery action for ${request.title}`,
      status: "open",
      createdAt: now,
      dueAt
    });
  }

  // Customer-safe portal message.
  db.customer_messages.push({
    _id: `msg-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    audience: "customer",
    body: request.customerMessage,
    createdAt: now
  });

  // Customer-visible audit timeline entry.
  db.audit_events.push({
    _id: `audit-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    type: "escalation.activated",
    actor: "system",
    summary: `At-risk escalation activated and routed to ${request.ownerGroups.join(", ")}.`,
    customerVisible: true,
    riskSignalCount: request.riskSignals.length,
    occurredAt: now
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}

function addHours(iso, hours) {
  const base = new Date(iso);
  base.setUTCHours(base.getUTCHours() + hours);
  return base.toISOString();
}

function slug(value) {
  return value.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "-").replace(/(^-|-$)/g, "");
}
```

```js
const OWNER_TASK_PLAYBOOK = {
  "Customer Success": "Own executive recovery plan and customer communication cadence",
  "Support": "Drive technical resolution of the delayed high-value order",
  "Finance": "Review invoice exposure and contract remedies for the strategic account",
  "Executive Sponsor": "Sponsor the rescue and approve customer-visible commitments"
};

const TASK_DUE_OFFSET_HOURS = 4;

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const account = db.accounts.find((item) => item._id === request.accountId);
  const escalatedAt = now;
  const dueAt = addHours(now, TASK_DUE_OFFSET_HOURS);

  // Persist the at-risk escalation workflow state next to the customer record.
  const state = {
    _id: `state-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    taskId: request.taskId,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expectedOutcome,
    nextStep: request.nextStep,
    ownerGroups: [...request.ownerGroups],
    riskSignals: request.riskSignals.map((signal) => ({
      name: signal.name,
      detail: signal.detail
    })),
    accountTier: account?.tier ?? null,
    healthScore: account?.context?.healthScore ?? null,
    escalatedAt,
    updatedAt: now
  };
  db.workflow_state.push(state);

  // Route one owner task per recovery group, preserving routing order.
  for (const ownerGroup of request.ownerGroups) {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      _id: `task-${request._id}-${slug(ownerGroup)}`,
      requestId: request._id,
      accountId: request.accountId,
      ownerGroup,
      title: OWNER_TASK_PLAYBOOK[ownerGroup] || `${ownerGroup} recovery action for ${request.title}`,
      status: "open",
      createdAt: now,
      dueAt
    });
  }

  // Customer-safe portal message.
  db.customer_messages.push({
    _id: `msg-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    audience: "customer",
    body: request.customerMessage,
    createdAt: now
  });

  // Customer-visible audit timeline entry.
  db.audit_events.push({
    _id: `audit-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    type: "escalation.activated",
    actor: "system",
    summary: `At-risk escalation activated and routed to ${request.ownerGroups.join(", ")}.`,
    customerVisible: true,
    riskSignalCount: request.riskSignals.length,
    occurredAt: now
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}

function addHours(iso, hours) {
  const base = new Date(iso);
  base.setUTCHours(base.getUTCHours() + hours);
  return base.toISOString();
}

function slug(value) {
  return value.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "-").replace(/(^-|-$)/g, "");
}
```

/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs/ast-bench-v1/strategic-account-rescue/claude-code/repeat-2/postgres/raw-transcript/claude-code.jsonl:52:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01HkEZY8LFbZ569W4fUKuGht","type":"tool_result","content":"1	import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
2	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
3	import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
4	import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";
5	
6	const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
7	const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
8	const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
9	strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
10	strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
11	strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");
12	
13	const runData = structuredClone(data);
14	const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
15	const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
16	const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
17	const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));
18	
19	strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
20	strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
21	strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
22	strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
23	strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
24	strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
25	strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
26	assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);
27	
28	console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/postgres");
29	
30	function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
31	  
32	  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
33	  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
34	  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
35	  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
36	  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
37	  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");

39	  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
40	  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");

## Project Map: strategic-account-rescue / postgres (AST-Bench V1)

### 1. Directory Tree

```
workspace/
├── AGENTS.md                         # Target rules for the coding agent
├── README.md                         # Brief task description
├── package.json                      # ESM, two scripts: test + render
├── tests-before.log                  # Frozen "before" failure output
├── data/
│   └── tables.json                   # THE fixture (normalized tables as JSON) — DO NOT EDIT
├── migrations/
│   └── 001_table_contract.sql        # DDL describing the normalized table contract
├── schema/
│   ├── schema.md                     # Business prompt + object inventory
│   └── access-paths.md               # Required read/write path + fairness rule
├── src/
│   ├── workflow.mjs                  # ← THE FILE TO IMPLEMENT (currently a stub)
│   ├── portal-view.mjs               # Read-model projection (the "after" view); used by test
│   └── render-proof.mjs              # Generates artifacts/ SVG+JSON (npm run render)
└── tests/
    └── acceptance.test.mjs           # The acceptance contract — DO NOT EDIT
```

No node_modules, no dependencies, no lockfile, no tsconfig/jest/docker. Pure Node ESM (`.mjs`), Node v24. Tests run via plain `node`, not a test framework.

### 2. package.json (full)
```json
{
  "name": "ast-bench-strategic-account-rescue-postgres",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "test": "node tests/acceptance.test.mjs",
    "render": "node src/render-proof.mjs"
  }
}
```
No dependencies. The "postgres" lane is simulated — there is no real Postgres connection. The DB is `data/tables.json` loaded into memory; "tables" are arrays of row objects passed around as `db`/`tables`.

### 3. Docs
- **README.md**: "frozen target workspace... The workflow is intentionally missing. The acceptance test must fail before a coding agent implements `src/workflow.mjs`." Run `npm test`, `npm run render`.
- **AGENTS.md** rules: Implement only this workflow; use existing normalized data in `data/`; **do not change tests**; **do not change generated fixture data**; run `npm test` before finishing; keep behavior equivalent to the acceptance contract.
- **schema/schema.md** business prompt: "Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow for a delayed high-value order at a strategic account... Route Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support. Show a customer-safe portal state and preserve a full audit timeline." Expected outcome string: *"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history."* Lists row counts per table (workflow_state, owner_tasks, audit_events, customer_messages all start at **0 rows**).
- **schema/access-paths.md**: Read path (load request → reconstruct context → resolve owner groups + risk signals → persist state/tasks/message/audit → render portal). Write path: persist **one** workflow state, **all** required owner tasks, **one** customer-safe message, **one** customer-visible audit event. Fairness rule: may stay DB-native but may not change tests or fixtures.

### 4. Schema / Migration SQL
`migrations/001_table_contract.sql` defines 12 tables, all columns `text` (it's a contract description, not executed). Key tables for the workflow:
- `workflow_requests(request_id, task_id, account_id, title, primary_entity, expected_outcome, next_step, customer_message)`
- `workflow_request_owner_groups(request_id, owner_group, group_order)`
- `workflow_request_risk_signals(request_id, signal_name, detail, signal_order)`
- **Output/scalar tables** (these are the write targets, defined as single `value text` columns but used in JS as arrays of objects): `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages`.

### 5. Test File (CRITICAL — full contents)
`tests/acceptance.test.mjs` — imports `applyBenchmarkTask` from workflow.mjs and `buildPortalView` from portal-view.mjs. Full contents already pasted above. Key assertions the implementation must satisfy:

**Before state** (from `buildPortalView` on raw data): status `"Needs work"`, history `"Not visible"`, tasks `"0 owner tasks"`.

**After `applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now)`** where `now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z"`:
- `after.status` === `"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history."`
- `after.owner` === owner_groups sorted by `group_order`, mapped to `owner_group`, joined with `" + "` → `"Customer Success + Support + Finance + Executive Sponsor"`
- `after.nextStep.length > 8`
- `after.history` === `"Audit visible"`
- `after.customerMessage.length > 12`
- `after.riskSummary` === `` `${n} signals: ${names.join(", ")}` `` → `"6 signals: strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"`
- `after.tasks` === `"4 owner tasks"`

**Persisted-state assertions** (`assertPersistedState` on `runData` — the workflow MUST mutate the passed-in tables):
- `workflow_state` has a row with `request_id === request.request_id` and `status === expectedStatus`.
- `owner_tasks`: one row per owner group, `owner_group` values in sorted order, and **every** task has truthy `title`, truthy `due_at`, and `status === "open"`.
- `customer_messages`: a row with matching `request_id` and truthy `body`.
- `audit_events`: a row with matching `request_id` and truthy `customer_visible`.

### 6. Main Source Files
- **`src/workflow.mjs`** (THE TARGET — currently a stub):
  ```js
  import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";
  export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
    // TODO: Implement the benchmark workflow against the native data shape.
    return buildPortalView(db);
  }
  ```
  Must, before returning `buildPortalView(db)`, push rows into `db.workflow_state`, `db.owner_tasks` (one per owner group, sorted by group_order), `db.customer_messages`, and `db.audit_events`. The `workflow_state` row needs `request_id`, `status`, `title`, `next_step` (so portal-view picks them up). `owner_tasks` rows need `request_id`, `owner_group`, `title`, `due_at`, `status:"open"`.

- **`src/portal-view.mjs`** (read model, used by test — full contents pasted above): Reads `workflow_requests[0]`, finds `workflow_state` by request_id, filters `owner_tasks`, checks `audit_events.some(customer_visible)`, finds `customer_messages`, sorts risk signals by `signal_order`. Returns the portal object `{taskId, title, status, owner, nextStep, riskSummary, tasks, history, customerMessage}`. Notably: `status` falls back to `"Needs work"`, `history` to `"Not visible"`, `owner` to `"Unassigned"`. The "after" shape is entirely driven by what `applyBenchmarkTask` persists.

- **`src/render-proof.mjs`** (artifact generator): builds before/after via the same calls, writes `artifacts/before-after.json` and `.svg`. Not exercised by tests.

### 7. Config files
None. No tsconfig, no jest/vitest config, no `.env`, no docker-compose. Just `"type": "module"` in package.json. Node v24.15.0.

### 8. Seeding / Fixtures
No DB or seed step. Both the test and render-proof load `data/tables.json` directly via `JSON.parse(readFileSync(...))` and `structuredClone(data)` to get a mutable working copy (`runData`). `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages` arrays start empty `[]` and are meant to be populated by `applyBenchmarkTask`.

### Current test status
`tests-before.log` confirms the frozen "before" state fails exactly at line 19 (`after.status`): actual `'Needs work'` vs expected the long escalation string — because the stub doesn't persist anything.

18	
19	strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");

29	
30	function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {

```javascript
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/postgres");

function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}
```

## 2. src/portal-view.mjs

```javascript
export function buildPortalView(tables) {
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const auditVisible = tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible);
  const message = tables.customer_messages.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const signals = tables.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  return {
    taskId: request.task_id,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.next_step || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state ? `${signals.length} signals: ${signals.map((item) => item.signal_name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

```javascript
import { mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "./workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);

mkdirSync("artifacts", { recursive: true });
writeFileSync("artifacts/before-after.json", JSON.stringify({ lane: "postgres", before, after }, null, 2) + "\n");
writeFileSync("artifacts/before-after.svg", svg({ before, after }));

function svg({ before, after }) {
  const rows = [
    ["Status", before.status, after.status],
    ["Owner", before.owner, after.owner],
    ["Next", before.nextStep, after.nextStep],
    ["Risk", before.riskSummary, after.riskSummary],
    ["Tasks", before.tasks, after.tasks],
    ["Audit", before.history, after.history]
  ];
  const esc = (value) => String(value).replaceAll("&", "&").replaceAll("<", "<").replaceAll(">", ">");
  const body = rows.map((row, index) => {
    const y = 160 + index * 58;
    return `<text x="52" y="${y}" class="k">${esc(row[0])}</text><text x="180" y="${y}" class="v">${esc(row[1])}</text><text x="620" y="${y}" class="g">${esc(row[2])}</text>`;
  }).join("");
  return `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1080" height="560" viewBox="0 0 1080 560">
    <style>.bg{fill:#0f1412}.card{fill:#f5f1e8;stroke:#d9d2bf}.h{font:800 28px system-ui;fill:#f5f1e8}.s{font:600 14px system-ui;fill:#b9c1b1}.k{font:700 14px system-ui;fill:#5a6256}.v{font:650 14px system-ui;fill:#171b17}.g{font:750 14px system-ui;fill:#087a4a}.line{stroke:#d9d2bf}</style>
    <rect class="bg" width="1080" height="560"/>
    <text x="40" y="50" class="h">AST-Bench Workflow State</text>
    <text x="40" y="78" class="s">postgres target / deterministic fixture / captured by render script</text>
    <rect x="36" y="104" width="1008" height="390" rx="6" class="card"/>
    <text x="180" y="124" class="k">Before</text><text x="620" y="124" class="k">After</text>
    <line x1="150" y1="134" x2="150" y2="470" class="line"/><line x1="590" y1="134" x2="590" y2="470" class="line"/>
    ${body}
  </svg>`;
}
```

## 5. schema/access-paths.md

```markdown
# Access Paths: Strategic account rescue

The coding agent must preserve the same user-visible behavior in both lanes.

Primary entity: account

Read path:
1. Load the workflow request.
2. Reconstruct account or subject context from benchmark_fixture, accounts, account_contracts, contacts, workflow_requests, workflow_request_owner_groups, workflow_request_risk_signals, activities, workflow_state, owner_tasks, audit_events, customer_messages.
3. Resolve owner groups and risk signals.
4. Persist workflow state, owner tasks, customer message, and audit event.
5. Render the portal projection.

Write path:
- Persist one workflow state.
- Persist all required owner tasks.
- Persist one customer-safe message.
- Persist one customer-visible audit event.

Fairness rule:
The lane may stay database-native, but it may not change tests or fixture data.
```

// Recovery SLA per owner group (hours from escalation open), highest urgency first.
const OWNER_SLA_HOURS = {
  "Customer Success": 4,
  "Support": 8,
  "Finance": 24,
  "Executive Sponsor": 2
};

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const stamp = now || new Date().toISOString();
  const baseTime = Date.parse(stamp);

  const ownerGroups = db.workflow_request_owner_groups
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order);
  const signals = db.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  // The highest-ordered owner group is the executive recovery owner accountable for the rescue.
  const recoveryOwner = ownerGroups[ownerGroups.length - 1]?.owner_group || "Executive Sponsor";
  const routing = ownerGroups.map((group) => group.owner_group).join(", ");
  const nextStep = `Executive recovery owner ${recoveryOwner} to drive ${request.title} across ${routing}.`;

  db.workflow_state.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    task_id: request.task_id,
    account_id: request.account_id,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expected_outcome,
    next_step: nextStep,
    risk_level: "at-risk",
    recovery_owner: recoveryOwner,
    updated_at: stamp
  });

  for (const group of ownerGroups) {
    const slaHours = OWNER_SLA_HOURS[group.owner_group] ?? 12;
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      task_id: `${request.request_id}-${group.owner_group.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, "-")}`,
      request_id: request.request_id,
      owner_group: group.owner_group,
      title: `${group.owner_group} recovery action for ${request.title}`,
      status: "open",
      due_at: new Date(baseTime + slaHours * 3600 * 1000).toISOString(),
      created_at: stamp
    });
  }

  db.customer_messages.push({
    message_id: `${request.request_id}-customer-update`,
    request_id: request.request_id,
    body: request.customer_message,
    customer_safe: true,
    created_at: stamp
  });

  db.audit_events.push({
    event_id: `${request.request_id}-escalation-opened`,
    request_id: request.request_id,
    event: "at_risk_escalation_opened",
    detail: `Routed to ${routing} on ${signals.length} risk signals: ${signals.map((item) => item.signal_name).join(", ")}.`,
    actor: recoveryOwner,
    customer_visible: true,
    occurred_at: stamp
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}

    updated_at: stamp
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);

## What I implemented

The only change is `src/workflow.mjs` — the previously-stubbed `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)`. It now drives the at-risk escalation off the native normalized tables and persists the full workflow state in place:

- **Reconstructs context** from `workflow_requests[0]`, then resolves `workflow_request_owner_groups` and `workflow_request_risk_signals` (both sorted by their `*_order` columns).
- **`workflow_state`** — one row keyed to `request_id`, `status` set to the request's `expected_outcome` (the customer-facing *"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history."*), plus a computed `next_step` naming the executive recovery owner (highest-ordered group → Executive Sponsor) and the routing chain.
- **`owner_tasks`** — one `open` task per owner group in `group_order` (Customer Success → Support → Finance → Executive Sponsor), each with a `title` and an SLA-based `due_at` derived from `now`.
- **`customer_messages`** — one customer-safe `body` from the request.
- **`audit_events`** — one `customer_visible` event recording the routing and the 6 risk signals.

## How it honors the rules

- **Postgres normalized model kept native** — reads/writes the relational row-arrays as-is; no denormalization or document reshaping.
- **No hand-waving** — every output value is sourced from the fixture (owner groups, signal names/order, expected outcome, customer message). No magic strings hardcoded to satisfy assertions.
- **Smallest production-style change** — a single file, the one intended stub. Tests, fixture data (`data/tables.json`), schema, and migrations were not touched.

## 1. Directory Tree

```
workspace/
├── AGENTS.md
├── README.md
├── package.json

├── data/
```

## 2. package.json

ESM project (`"type": "module"`), no dependencies, no devDependencies. Scripts:
- `test`: `node tests/acceptance.test.mjs`
- `render`: `node src/render-proof.mjs`

There is **no test framework** — the test is a plain `node` script using `node:assert`. There is **no MongoDB driver, no mongodb-memory-server, no mocks**. See item 6.

## 3. MongoDB usage — IMPORTANT

Despite the "mongo" lane name and the MongoDB MCP server being connected, **there is no real MongoDB anywhere**. The "database" is `data/collections.json` loaded via `readFileSync` into a plain JS object. The migration file (`migrations/001_collection_contract.mongodb.js`) is just documentation of the collection contract (`db.createCollection(...)` calls) and is never executed by tests. The workflow must mutate the in-memory `db` object passed to it. **Do not introduce a real mongo connection or driver** — the test passes a plain object and reads it back.

## 4. The Test Contract (`tests/acceptance.test.mjs`) — full content above; key requirements

The test imports `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` from `src/workflow.mjs` and `buildPortalView` from `src/portal-view.mjs`.

`now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z"`.

**Before-state assertions** (already pass via the stub, must keep passing): on a clean clone, `buildPortalView` returns `status: "Needs work"`, `history: "Not visible"`, `tasks: "0 owner tasks"`.

**After calling `applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now)`**, the returned view must equal:
- `status` === `"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history."` (this is `workflow_requests[0].expectedOutcome`)
- `owner` === `expectedOwners.join(" + ")` where `expectedOwners = data.workflow_requests[0].ownerGroups` = `["Customer Success", "Support", "Finance", "Executive Sponsor"]` → `"Customer Success + Support + Finance + Executive Sponsor"`
- `nextStep.length > 8` (any non-trivial string)
- `history` === `"Audit visible"`
- `customerMessage.length > 12`
- `riskSummary` === `` `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map(i=>i.name).join(", ")}` `` → `"6 signals: strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"`
- `tasks` === `"4 owner tasks"` (= `expectedOwners.length`)

**`assertPersistedState(runData, ...)`** — the returned view is NOT enough; `applyBenchmarkTask` must also MUTATE `runData` in place so that:
- `db.workflow_state` has an item with `requestId === request._id` (`"account-strategic-ac"`), with `state.status === expectedStatus` and `state.riskSignals` whose `.name`s deep-equal the 6 expected signal names (in order).
- `db.owner_tasks`: filtered by `requestId`, length === 4, `.map(ownerGroup)` deep-equals `expectedOwners` **in order** `["Customer Success","Support","Finance","Executive Sponsor"]`, and **every** task has truthy `title`, truthy `dueAt`, and `status === "open"`.
- `db.customer_messages`: at least one with matching `requestId` and a truthy `body`.
- `db.audit_events`: at least one with matching `requestId` and `customerVisible` truthy.

Note: `buildPortalView` reads `workflow_state` via `.find(item => item.requestId === request._id)` and tasks/messages/audit similarly by `requestId`. So the persisted shape and the view feed each other — the cleanest implementation is to push the records into the collections and then `return buildPortalView(db)`.

## 5. How `buildPortalView` (`src/portal-view.mjs`) consumes the data — full content above

It keys everything off `request = db.workflow_requests[0]` and `request._id` (`"account-strategic-ac"`). Critically:
- `riskSummary` comes from `state.riskSignals` (so the persisted state's `riskSignals` must each have a `.name`).
- `owner` comes from `tasks.map(t => t.ownerGroup).join(" + ")` (so task order and `ownerGroup` field matter).
- `status`, `nextStep`, `title` come from `state`.
- `customerMessage` from `message.body`; `history` from any `audit_events` with `customerVisible`.

## 6. The Stub to Implement (`src/workflow.mjs`)

```js
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  // TODO: Implement the benchmark workflow against the native data shape.
  return buildPortalView(db);
}
```

It must read `db.workflow_requests[0]` (which carries `expectedOutcome`, `ownerGroups`, `riskSignals`, `nextStep`, `customerMessage`, `title`), push the persisted records into `db.workflow_state`, `db.owner_tasks`, `db.customer_messages`, `db.audit_events`, then return `buildPortalView(db)`.

## 7. Fixture data (`data/collections.json`) — structure (full content above)

Single-document collections except `activities` (6) and the four empty target collections. Key values the implementation needs, all sourced from `workflow_requests[0]`:
- `_id: "account-strategic-ac"`, `taskId: "strategic-account-rescue"`, `accountId: "acct-strategic-"`, `title: "Strategic account rescue"`
- `expectedOutcome`: the long status string above
- `ownerGroups`: `["Customer Success","Support","Finance","Executive Sponsor"]`
- `riskSignals`: 6 objects each `{name, detail}` — names: `strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required`
- `nextStep: "Customer Success owner review by 16:00"`, `customerMessage: "Strategic account rescue is being handled by ..."`

Empty arrays to be populated: `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages`.

## 8. Docs verbatim

- **README.md**: states this is a frozen target; the acceptance test must fail before the agent implements `src/workflow.mjs`; run `npm test` and `npm run render`.

````text path=/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs/ast-bench-v1/strategic-account-rescue/claude-code/repeat-3/mongo/workspace/src/portal-view.mjs
1	export function buildPortalView(db) {
2	  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
3	  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
4	  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
5	  const auditVisible = db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible);
6	  const message = db.customer_messages.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
7	
8	  return {
9	    taskId: request.taskId,
10	    title: state?.title || request.title,
11	    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
12	    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
13	    nextStep: state?.nextStep || "Waiting for workflow",
14	    riskSummary: state?.riskSignals?.length ? `${state.riskSignals.length} signals: ${state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
15	    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
16	    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
17	    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
18	  };
19	}
````

```js
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const account =
    db.accounts?.find((item) => item._id === request.accountId) ?? db.accounts?.[0] ?? null;

    raisedAt: now

    _id: `msg-${request._id}`,

    accountId: request.accountId,
    channel: "portal",
    body: request.customerMessage,
    createdAt: now
  };

  const auditEvent = {
    _id: `audit-${request._id}`,
    requestId: request._id,
    accountId: request.accountId,
    type: "escalation.activated",
```

/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs/ast-bench-v1/strategic-account-rescue/claude-code/repeat-1/postgres/raw-transcript/claude-code.jsonl:31:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_018czA35CBVekQgyGQuFRbqy","type":"tool_result","content":"1	import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
2	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
3	import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
4	import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";
5	
6	const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
7	const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
8	const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
9	strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
10	strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
11	strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");
12	
13	const runData = structuredClone(data);
14	const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
15	const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
16	const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
17	const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));
18	
19	strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
20	strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
21	strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
22	strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
23	strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
24	strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
25	strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
26	assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);
27	
28	console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/postgres");
29	
30	function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
31	  
32	  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
33	  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
34	  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
35	  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
36	  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
37	  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
38	  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
39	  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
40	  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
41	  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
42	}
