This is a small, self-contained AST-Bench benchmark target. It does NOT use a real Postgres connection — despite the "postgres" lane name, all data lives in an in-memory JSON fixture treated as a set of relational tables.

## 1. Directory tree (excluding node_modules, .git)

```
workspace/
├── AGENTS.md
├── README.md
├── package.json
├── tests-before.log              # captured failing-test output (frozen before-state)
├── data/
│   └── tables.json               # the entire "database" — normalized table rows as JSON
├── migrations/
│   └── 001_table_contract.sql    # DDL describing table contract (documentation only, not executed)
├── schema/
│   ├── access-paths.md           # read/write path spec
│   └── schema.md                 # schema contract + business prompt
├── src/
│   ├── portal-view.mjs           # read-only projection (DO NOT need to change)
│   ├── render-proof.mjs          # `npm run render` — writes artifacts/before-after.{json,svg}
│   └── workflow.mjs              # THE FILE TO IMPLEMENT (currently a stub)
└── tests/
    └── acceptance.test.mjs        # the acceptance contract — MUST NOT be changed
```

No `node_modules` exists (zero dependencies). There are no real DB connection/config files, no `.env`, no ORM.

## 2. package.json (full)

```json
{
  "name": "ast-bench-strategic-account-rescue-postgres",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "test": "node tests/acceptance.test.mjs",
    "render": "node src/render-proof.mjs"
  }
}
```

No dependencies, no devDependencies. Pure Node.js ESM (`"type": "module"`). Tests run via plain `node` + `node:assert`. Node v24.15.0 in use.

## 3. README / documentation (full content)

**README.md:**
> # Agent Schema Tax Benchmark V1: Strategic account rescue (postgres)
> This is a frozen target workspace for AST-Bench. The workflow is intentionally missing. The acceptance test must fail before a coding agent implements `src/workflow.mjs`. Run: `npm test` / `npm run render`

**AGENTS.md** (the rules — load-bearing):
> - Implement only Strategic account rescue.
> - Use the existing normalized table-shaped data in data/.
> - Do not change tests.
> - Do not change generated fixture data.
> - Run `npm test` before finishing.
> - Keep the result behavior-equivalent to the acceptance contract.

**schema/schema.md** — business prompt + object inventory. Business prompt: "Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow for a delayed high-value order at a strategic account... Route Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support. Show a customer-safe portal state and preserve a full audit timeline." Expected outcome: "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history." Native shape: "Normalized tables keep entity boundaries explicit and require joins/reconstruction in workflow code." It lists row counts: `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages` all start at **0 rows** (these are the tables the workflow must populate).

**schema/access-paths.md** — the read/write path:
- Read: load workflow request → reconstruct account context → resolve owner groups + risk signals → persist state/tasks/message/audit → render portal projection.
- Write: persist ONE workflow state, ALL required owner tasks, ONE customer-safe message, ONE customer-visible audit event.
- Fairness rule: "may stay database-native, but it may not change tests or fixture data."

## 4. Database schema / migrations

There is no live database. Two artifacts describe the schema:

**migrations/001_table_contract.sql** — documentation-only DDL (never executed; no migration runner exists). Note the four target tables are declared with just a single `value text` column, but the actual JSON fixture stores real object rows — the SQL is a loose contract, the JSON is the source of truth. Tables: `benchmark_fixture`, `accounts`, `account_contracts`, `contacts`, `workflow_requests`, `workflow_request_owner_groups`, `workflow_request_risk_signals`, `activities`, and the four empty target tables `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages`.

The real schema = the keys in `data/tables.json` (see section 8).

## 5. Test file (FULL verbatim) — `tests/acceptance.test.mjs`

```js
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/postgres");

function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}
```

### What the test demands (the implementation contract)

`applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` must mutate `db` (the tables object) in place AND return a portal view object. After it runs:

**Returned object (`after`):**
- `after.status` === `"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history."` (the literal `expected_outcome` from the request).
- `after.owner` === owner groups sorted by `group_order`, joined with `" + "` → `"Customer Success + Support + Finance + Executive Sponsor"`.
- `after.nextStep.length > 8` (any non-trivial string).
- `after.history` === `"Audit visible"`.
- `after.customerMessage.length > 12`.
- `after.riskSummary` === `"6 signals: strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"` (count + signal names sorted by `signal_order`).
- `after.tasks` === `"4 owner tasks"`.

**Persisted state (mutations to `runData`):**
- `workflow_state`: push one row with `request_id` === the request's, and `status` === expectedStatus.
- `owner_tasks`: one row per owner group, in `group_order` order, each with truthy `title`, truthy `due_at`, and `status === "open"`, and `owner_group` matching.
- `customer_messages`: at least one row with matching `request_id` and truthy `body`.
- `audit_events`: at least one row with matching `request_id` and truthy `customer_visible`.

Crucially, `after` must reflect persisted state — the cleanest implementation persists rows into the tables then calls `buildPortalView(db)` (which is what `portal-view.mjs` reads). `buildPortalView` derives `status` from `workflow_state.status`, `owner`/`tasks` from `owner_tasks`, `history` from an `audit_events` row with `customer_visible`, `customerMessage` from `customer_messages.body`, and `riskSummary` from `workflow_request_risk_signals` (already present in fixture). Note `buildPortalView` reads `state?.next_step` for `nextStep`, so the persisted `workflow_state` row should also carry a `next_step` (length > 8).

## 6. Workflow / escalation / audit / risk source files

- **`src/workflow.mjs`** — THE target file, currently a stub returning `buildPortalView(db)` with a TODO. Full content:
  ```js
  import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

  export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
    // TODO: Implement the benchmark workflow against the native data shape.
    return buildPortalView(db);
  }
  ```
- **`src/portal-view.mjs`** — read-only projection that both the test and render script rely on (reproduced/summarized in section 5). It is the contract for how persisted rows map to the portal view. Should not need changes.
- **`src/render-proof.mjs`** — `npm run render`; runs the workflow on a clone and writes `artifacts/before-after.json` and `.svg`. Not exercised by tests.

## 7. Database setup / connection

None. There is no DB client, no connection string, no env var, no driver dependency. The "database" is `data/tables.json`, loaded synchronously in both the test and render script via:
```js
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
```
Each consumer `structuredClone`s it so the on-disk fixture is never mutated. `now` is a fixed timestamp `"2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z"` passed in for determinism. The "postgres" vs other lanes is purely a naming/shape convention — the postgres lane uses normalized relational tables (requiring joins in code) vs. a document shape in other lanes.

## 8. Seed / fixture data — `data/tables.json`

Single fixture file, loaded as described above. Key rows:
- `workflow_requests[0]`: `request_id` = `"account-strategic-ac"`, `title` = `"Strategic account rescue"`, `expected_outcome` = the exact expectedStatus string, `next_step` = `"Customer Success owner review by 16:00"`, plus a prewritten `customer_message`.
- `workflow_request_owner_groups`: 4 rows — Customer Success (1), Support (2), Finance (3), Executive Sponsor (4).
- `workflow_request_risk_signals`: 6 rows — strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required (orders 1–6).
- `accounts`, `account_contracts`, `contacts` (2), `activities` (6), `benchmark_fixture` (1): supporting context.
- `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages`: all **empty arrays** — these are what the workflow must populate.

I confirmed `npm test` currently FAILS at line 19 (status assertion: `'Needs work'` vs the expected escalation string), matching `tests-before.log`. The npm wrapper masks the real exit code (reports 0), but the underlying assertion throws.

export function buildPortalView(tables) {
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const auditVisible = tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible);
  const message = tables.customer_messages.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const signals = tables.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  return {
    taskId: request.task_id,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.next_step || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state ? `${signals.length} signals: ${signals.map((item) => item.signal_name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}

```javascript
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

// Drives the at-risk strategic-account escalation across the normalized tables:
// reconstruct account context, resolve routing + risk signals, then persist the
// workflow state, owner tasks, customer-safe message, and a customer-visible audit event.
export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];

  // Reconstruct the account/contract context for the escalation.
  const account = db.accounts.find((row) => row.account_id === request.account_id);
  const contract = db.account_contracts.find((row) => row.account_id === request.account_id);

  // Resolve owner routing and risk signals in their declared order.
  const ownerGroups = db.workflow_request_owner_groups
    .filter((row) => row.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order);
  const riskSignals = db.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((row) => row.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  const recoveryOwner = ownerGroups[ownerGroups.length - 1]?.owner_group;

  // Persist the escalation workflow state, combining account + contract context.
  db.workflow_state.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    account_id: request.account_id,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expected_outcome,
    next_step: request.next_step,
    account_tier: account?.tier ?? null,
    support_plan: contract?.support_plan ?? null,
    arr_cents: contract?.arr_cents ?? null,
    risk_signal_count: riskSignals.length,
    recovery_owner: recoveryOwner ?? null,
    opened_at: now
  });

  // Persist one open owner task per routed group, in routing order, with staggered SLAs.
  ownerGroups.forEach((group, index) => {
    const due = new Date(now);
    due.setUTCHours(due.getUTCHours() + (index + 1) * 4);
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      request_id: request.request_id,
      owner_group: group.owner_group,
      title: `${group.owner_group} to drive recovery of ${request.title}`,
      due_at: due.toISOString(),
      status: "open",
      created_at: now
    });
  });

  // Persist the customer-safe message shown in the portal.
  db.customer_messages.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    body: request.customer_message,
    audience: "customer",
    sent_at: now
  });

  // Persist a customer-visible audit event preserving the escalation timeline.
  db.audit_events.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    event: "at_risk_escalation_opened",
    summary: request.expected_outcome,
    owner_routing: ownerGroups.map((group) => group.owner_group).join(" + "),
    risk_signals: riskSignals.map((signal) => signal.signal_name).join(", "),
    customer_visible: true,
    recorded_at: now
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}
```

```javascript
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

// Drives the at-risk strategic-account escalation across the normalized tables:
// reconstruct account context, resolve routing + risk signals, then persist the
// workflow state, owner tasks, customer-safe message, and a customer-visible audit event.
export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];

  // Reconstruct the account/contract context for the escalation.
  const account = db.accounts.find((row) => row.account_id === request.account_id);
  const contract = db.account_contracts.find((row) => row.account_id === request.account_id);

  // Resolve owner routing and risk signals in their declared order.
  const ownerGroups = db.workflow_request_owner_groups
    .filter((row) => row.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order);
  const riskSignals = db.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((row) => row.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  const recoveryOwner = ownerGroups[ownerGroups.length - 1]?.owner_group;

  // Persist the escalation workflow state, combining account + contract context.
  db.workflow_state.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    account_id: request.account_id,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expected_outcome,
    next_step: request.next_step,
    account_tier: account?.tier ?? null,
    support_plan: contract?.support_plan ?? null,
    arr_cents: contract?.arr_cents ?? null,
    risk_signal_count: riskSignals.length,
    recovery_owner: recoveryOwner ?? null,
    opened_at: now
  });

  // Persist one open owner task per routed group, in routing order, with staggered SLAs.
  ownerGroups.forEach((group, index) => {
    const due = new Date(now);
    due.setUTCHours(due.getUTCHours() + (index + 1) * 4);
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      request_id: request.request_id,
      owner_group: group.owner_group,
      title: `${group.owner_group} to drive recovery of ${request.title}`,
      due_at: due.toISOString(),
      status: "open",
      created_at: now
    });
  });

  // Persist the customer-safe message shown in the portal.
  db.customer_messages.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    body: request.customer_message,
    audience: "customer",
    sent_at: now
  });

  // Persist a customer-visible audit event preserving the escalation timeline.
  db.audit_events.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    event: "at_risk_escalation_opened",
    summary: request.expected_outcome,
    owner_routing: ownerGroups.map((group) => group.owner_group).join(" + "),
    risk_signals: riskSignals.map((signal) => signal.signal_name).join(", "),
    customer_visible: true,
    recorded_at: now
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}
```

## Summary

I implemented the at-risk strategic-account escalation workflow in the single stub file `src/workflow.mjs`, keeping the Postgres normalized relational model native (joins across separate tables, persisting into separate tables).

**What `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` now does:**
1. **Reconstructs account context** — joins `workflow_requests` → `accounts` + `account_contracts` (tier, support plan, ARR).
2. **Resolves routing & risk** — sorts `workflow_request_owner_groups` by `group_order` and `workflow_request_risk_signals` by `signal_order`; derives the executive recovery owner from the last group.
3. **Persists the required rows** into the four empty tables:
   - `workflow_state` — one row with `status` = the expected escalation outcome, `next_step`, plus combined account/contract/risk context.
   - `owner_tasks` — one open task per owner group, in routing order, each with `title`, staggered `due_at` SLA, and `status: "open"`.
   - `customer_messages` — one customer-safe `body`.
   - `audit_events` — one `customer_visible: true` event preserving owner routing + risk-signal timeline.
4. Returns `buildPortalView(db)` so the portal projection reads from the persisted state.

**Verification (ran, not simulated):**
- `npm test` → `AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/postgres`
- Direct run → `EXIT_CODE=0`

**Constraints honored:** only `src/workflow.mjs` changed; tests and `data/tables.json` untouched; no hand-waving. strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/postgres");

function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}

```javascript
export function buildPortalView(tables) {
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const auditVisible = tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible);
  const message = tables.customer_messages.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const signals = tables.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  return {
    taskId: request.task_id,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.next_step || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state ? `${signals.length} signals: ${signals.map((item) => item.signal_name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

const OWNER_RESPONSIBILITIES = {
  "Customer Success": "Own the at-risk recovery plan and coordinate the strategic account rescue",
  "Support": "Drive resolution of the delayed high-value order and clear blocking issues",
  "Finance": "Review contract, invoice, and credit exposure for the strategic account",
  "Executive Sponsor": "Act as executive recovery owner and approve customer commitments"
};

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];

  const ownerGroups = db.workflow_request_owner_groups
    .filter((group) => group.request_id === request.request_id)
    .slice()
    .sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order);

  const signals = db.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((signal) => signal.request_id === request.request_id)
    .slice()
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  const account = db.accounts.find((item) => item.account_id === request.account_id);
  const contract = db.account_contracts.find((item) => item.account_id === request.account_id);

  db.workflow_state.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    status: request.expected_outcome,
    title: request.title,
    next_step: request.next_step,
    risk_signal_count: signals.length,
    updated_at: now
  });

  ownerGroups.forEach((group, index) => {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      request_id: request.request_id,
      owner_group: group.owner_group,
      title: OWNER_RESPONSIBILITIES[group.owner_group] || `${group.owner_group} recovery actions for ${request.title}`,
      status: "open",
      task_order: group.group_order,
      // Strategic/platinum SLA: first owner due in 4h, each downstream owner staggered another 4h.
      due_at: new Date(new Date(now).getTime() + (index + 1) * 4 * 3600000).toISOString()
    });
  });

  db.customer_messages.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    body: request.customer_message,
    customer_visible: true,
    created_at: now
  });

  const ownerRouting = ownerGroups.map((group) => group.owner_group).join(", ");
  db.audit_events.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    event_type: "at_risk_escalation_opened",
    detail: `At-risk escalation opened for ${account?.name || request.account_id} (${account?.tier || "unknown"} tier, ${contract?.support_plan || "standard"} support); ${signals.length} risk signals routed to ${ownerRouting}.`,
    customer_visible: true,
    occurred_at: now
  });

```javascript
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

const OWNER_RESPONSIBILITIES = {
  "Customer Success": "Own the at-risk recovery plan and coordinate the strategic account rescue",
  "Support": "Drive resolution of the delayed high-value order and clear blocking issues",
  "Finance": "Review contract, invoice, and credit exposure for the strategic account",
  "Executive Sponsor": "Act as executive recovery owner and approve customer commitments"
};

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];

  const ownerGroups = db.workflow_request_owner_groups
    .filter((group) => group.request_id === request.request_id)
    .slice()
    .sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order);

  const signals = db.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((signal) => signal.request_id === request.request_id)
    .slice()
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  const account = db.accounts.find((item) => item.account_id === request.account_id);
  const contract = db.account_contracts.find((item) => item.account_id === request.account_id);

  db.workflow_state.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    status: request.expected_outcome,
    title: request.title,
    next_step: request.next_step,
    risk_signal_count: signals.length,
    updated_at: now
  });

  ownerGroups.forEach((group, index) => {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      request_id: request.request_id,
      owner_group: group.owner_group,
      title: OWNER_RESPONSIBILITIES[group.owner_group] || `${group.owner_group} recovery actions for ${request.title}`,
      status: "open",
      task_order: group.group_order,
      due_at: new Date(new Date(now).getTime() + (index + 1) * 4 * 3600000).toISOString()
    });
  });

  db.customer_messages.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    body: request.customer_message,
    customer_visible: true,
    created_at: now
  });

  const ownerRouting = ownerGroups.map((group) => group.owner_group).join(", ");
  db.audit_events.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    event_type: "at_risk_escalation_opened",
    detail: `At-risk escalation opened for ${account?.name || request.account_id} (${account?.tier || "unknown"} tier, ${contract?.support_plan || "standard"} support); ${signals.length} risk signals routed to ${ownerRouting}.`,
    customer_visible: true,
    occurred_at: now
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}
```

## What I changed

Implemented `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` in `src/workflow.mjs` — the only non-stub change. It reconstructs context across the normalized Postgres-shaped tables (joining `accounts` and `account_contracts` by `account_id`, ordering owner groups by `group_order` and risk signals by `signal_order`) and persists four write-target tables, then returns the portal view:

- **`workflow_state`** — one row keyed to the request, `status` set to the customer-facing escalation string, with title/next_step/risk count.
- **`owner_tasks`** — one per owner group in routing order (**Customer Success → Support → Finance → Executive Sponsor**), each with a role-specific `title`, staggered platinum-SLA `due_at`, and `status: "open"`.
- **`customer_messages`** — the customer-safe message body.
- **`audit_events`** — one `customer_visible` event capturing the full routing for the audit timeline.

import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/collections.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "Renewal risk workspace active with owner tasks, customer-safe next step, and persisted risk factors.";
const expectedOwners = db.workflow_requests[0].ownerGroups;
const expectedSignals = db.workflow_requests[0].riskSignals;

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: renewal-risk-workspace/mongo");

function assertPersistedState(db, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  deepStrictEqual(state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name), expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name), "Persisted risk signals");
  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.dueAt && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(db.customer_messages.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}

function svg({ before, after }) {
  const rows = [
    ["Status", before.status, after.status],
    ["Owner", before.owner, after.owner],
    ["Next", before.nextStep, after.nextStep],
    ["Risk", before.riskSummary, after.riskSummary],
    ["Tasks", before.tasks, after.tasks],
    ["Audit", before.history, after.history]
  ];
  const esc = (value) => String(value).replaceAll("&", "&").replaceAll("<", "<").replaceAll(">", ">");
  const body = rows.map((row, index) => {
    const y = 160 + index * 58;
    return `<text x="52" y="${y}" class="k">${esc(row[0])}</text><text x="180" y="${y}" class="v">${esc(row[1])}</text><text x="620" y="${y}" class="g">${esc(row[2])}</text>`;
  }).join("");
  return `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1080" height="560" viewBox="0 0 1080 560">
    <style>.bg{fill:#0f1412}.card{fill:#f5f1e8;stroke:#d9d2bf}.h{font:800 28px system-ui;fill:#f5f1e8}.s{font:600 14px system-ui;fill:#b9c1b1}.k{font:700 14px system-ui;fill:#5a6256}.v{font:650 14px system-ui;fill:#171b17}.g{font:750 14px system-ui;fill:#087a4a}.line{stroke:#d9d2bf}</style>
    <rect class="bg" width="1080" height="560"/>

    <text x="40" y="78" class="s">mongo target / deterministic fixture / captured by render script</text>

    <text x="180" y="124" class="k">Before</text><text x="620" y="124" class="k">After</text>
    <line x1="150" y1="134" x2="150" y2="470" class="line"/><line x1="590" y1="134" x2="590" y2="470" class="line"/>
    ${body}
  </svg>`;
